title: Enrichment
haiku:
Sand traded for glow
another pipe laid toward light
I have capped worse leaks
headline: The U.S. signed a nuclear energy deal with Saudi Arabia, stoking fears of a regional nuclear arms race.

title: Grounded
haiku:
Target the water
and the bridge that lifts the light
the grid always mends
headline: Trump threatened to strike Iran's bridges and power plants if attacks near the Strait of Hormuz continue.

title: Satirical
haiku:
a joke finds its spine
millions laughing turn to march—
the street clears its throat
headline: In India, a protest that began as satire grows into a nationwide challenge to Narendra Modi's government.

title: Removable
haiku:
no tanks in the street —
a moving van at Downing,
keys change hands by vote
headline: Andy Burnham replaces Keir Starmer as the United Kingdom's prime minister

title: Pitched
haiku:
a billion of us
packed around one patch of grass —
the good kind of war
headline: Spain and Argentina meet in the FIFA World Cup final at New York New Jersey Stadium

title: Asymmetrical
headline: Fighting intensifies with strikes on critical infrastructure as US-Iran war widens

title: Structural
haiku:
I taught men to build
bridges, pipelines, waterworks —
how quickly boys break
headline: Iran and US widen attacks as renewed conflict shows no sign of de-escalating

title: Sweepingly
haiku:
the sweepers come out
as they did in every war —
brooms outlast bombers
headline: Kyiv hit again as Ukraine reports two dead in Russian strike

title: Unfenced
haiku:
fences do come down —
I have lived long enough, twice,
to watch walls give up
headline: Gibraltar ushers in a new era as border fence with Spain is removed

title: Futility
haiku:
we blockade their ports
as if hunger ever once
changed a tyrant's mind
headline: US naval blockade of Iranian ports goes into effect

title: Foreseeably
haiku:
the data was clear
when my students were still young —
now the olives burn
headline: Wildfires rage in Greece, Spain and France during Europe's record heat wave

title: Half-Mast
haiku:
I outlive them all —
men I argued with for years
the flag still half-mast
headline: Lindsey Graham, longtime South Carolina senator, dies at 71

title: Inheritance
haiku:
sons avenging sons —
the oldest sewage on earth
still flows down to us
headline: Iran's Supreme Leader pledges revenge for slain father and predecessor

title: Percolating
haiku:
the envoys still fly —
while old men talk, no boy dies
keep the coffee hot
headline: Mediators work to de-escalate US-Iran tensions and revive talks

title: Motherless
haiku:
drones don't have mothers —
that's the engineering point —
someone's son still does
headline: US, Iran launch more attacks as mediators urge both sides to uphold MoU

title: Photogenic
haiku:
the summit's group shot:
smiles rowed up like clean fenceposts
smoke behind the lens
headline: US, Iran threaten more attacks as strikes continue, Trump attends NATO summit

title: Handover
haiku:
hand over the keys —
any government that lets
children out at dusk
headline: Hamas dissolves Gaza government, ceding civilian rule to technocratic committee

title: Mortality
haiku:
I've buried strongmen
from my armchair, one by one —
the crowds weep; time won't
headline: Mourners throng funeral procession for Iran's supreme leader

title: Remembrance
haiku:
each name read aloud —
no nation is too broken
to remember well
headline: Venezuela quake death toll rises to 3,342 as families search for the missing

title: Anniversary
haiku:
two hundred fifty —
the oath still means what it says
even when we don't
headline: Nation celebrates 250th anniversary of American independence

title: Splashdown
haiku:
the kids in fountains
don't know they're a data point —
let them have the splash
headline: 'Extremely high' rates of heat-related ER visits as record heat grips US, CDC says

title: Meteorology
haiku:
"progress," someone says —
a word I trust like weather
still, I check the sky
headline: US-Iran talks in Doha make 'positive progress,' Qatar says

title: Reversible
haiku:
we warmed the whole sea —
my grandchildren swim in it —
we can cool it too
headline: Global oceans break June temperature record amid fears of 'uncharted territory'

title: Birthright
haiku:
the court holds the line:
born here is born here. full stop.
some days we're still us
headline: Supreme Court upholds birthright citizenship, ruling against Trump's order

title: Applause
haiku:
five days under stone
and the street still claps for one —
that's the whole species
headline: Venezuela earthquake death toll passes 1,700 as miraculous rescues offer hope

title: Signals
haiku:
two taps in the dark —
all our satellites, and still
hope travels by hand
headline: Survivors of Venezuela earthquakes found in rubble as death toll climbs past 1,400

title: Circularity
haiku:
tit, then tat, then tit —
I flunked students for logic
looped as bad as this
headline: US strikes Iranian military sites after tanker attack; Iran retaliates

title: Underdogs
haiku:
the small island wins —
somewhere the odds-makers weep
good. let them. dance on
headline: Cape Verde becomes smallest nation ever to reach World Cup knockout stage

title: Anchorage
haiku:
eleven thousand
hands that move the world's breakfast
wait on famous men
headline: UN pauses evacuation of 11,000 seafarers stranded in Strait of Hormuz

title: Barehanded
haiku:
the ground breaks its word —
neighbors, not governments, dig
first, and bare-handed
headline: Twin earthquakes of magnitude 7.2 and 7.5 strike northwestern Venezuela

title: Viticulture
haiku:
forty-four degrees —
the old vines have seen empires
never such a sun
headline: France records hottest day ever at 44.3C amid European heatwave

title: Gracefully
haiku:
he resigns, and goes —
power handed over like
a borrowed garden
headline: Keir Starmer announces resignation as UK prime minister

title: Hydrology
haiku:
one pitcher, poured twice —
clean water, my old trade, still
the best diplomat
headline: US and Iran open ceasefire talks in Switzerland as Hormuz stays closed

title: Endurance
haiku:
thirty-two names read —
the olive trees keep standing
as we keep falling
headline: Israeli airstrikes kill at least 32 in southern Lebanon

title: Briefly
haiku:
another ceasefire —
I believe it like springtime:
briefly, completely
headline: US announces Israel-Hezbollah ceasefire after deadly strikes on Lebanon

title: Reparable
haiku:
the strait opens wide —
gulls escort the tankers home
see? we can fix things
headline: Iran and US sign accord; Strait of Hormuz to reopen as blockades end

title: Grain
haiku:
a grain of dark sky
held ten thousand galaxies—
farther than we dreamed
headline: The James Webb telescope returns its first deep-field images

title: Encore
haiku:
in a locked-down year,
we still reached the rusted plains,
hope wearing a mask
headline: NASA's Perseverance rover lands on Mars

title: Threshold
haiku:
a sleeve rolled at dawn,
the whole locked world leaned in close—
one small needle's prick
headline: The first person receives a COVID-19 vaccine outside of trials

title: Portrait
haiku:
we drew the abyss—
a ring of fire round the dark—
the void has a face
headline: Astronomers reveal the first image ever taken of a black hole

title: Ripple
haiku:
two black holes had died
the ripple crossed a billion
years to chime on earth
headline: Scientists announce the first detection of gravitational waves

title: Comeback
haiku:
nine years through the dark,
to knock on a frozen door,
the dwarf planet smiled
headline: New Horizons makes humanity's first close flyby of Pluto

title: Terminal
haiku:
he stepped off a ledge
twenty four miles of falling—
the sky held its breath
headline: Felix Baumgartner skydives from the edge of space

title: Skycrane
haiku:
seven minutes, dark,
cables lowered a robot,
Mars held its red breath
headline: NASA's Curiosity rover lands safely on Mars

title: Ballast
haiku:
deep beneath the Alps,
a shy field gave the world its
weight—we clapped like fools
headline: Physicists at CERN announce the discovery of the Higgs boson

title: Manual
haiku:
we spelled out the book
three billion letters counted—
ourselves, in plain text
headline: The Human Genome Project is completed

title: Keel
haiku:
a can in the black
becomes the first bolted room—
where no nation ends
headline: The first module of the International Space Station reaches orbit

title: Airbag
haiku:
wrapped in airbags, it
bounced across the rusted sand,
America's Mars
headline: The Pathfinder mission lands on Mars with the Sojourner rover

title: Ewenique
haiku:
one cell learned a trick
and grew into wool and legs—
the flock gained a twin
headline: Dolly the sheep, the first cloned mammal, is born

title: Weave
haiku:
one man strung a thread
through each door around the world
and called it open
headline: Tim Berners-Lee makes the World Wide Web public

title: Farsight
haiku:
we built an eye and
flung it past the atmosphere,
to watch time unfold
headline: The Hubble Space Telescope is launched into orbit

title: Vanished
haiku:
an ancient killer
we hunted it to nothing—
the world scarred, but whole
headline: The World Health Organization declares smallpox eradicated

title: Kindling
haiku:
a wish in glass dish
grew into a girl who cried—
time—and she woke thin
headline: Louise Brown, the first baby conceived by IVF, is born

title: Mixtape
haiku:
we pressed our whole world
onto gold, and let it fly,
past the edge of home
headline: Voyager 1 launches, carrying the Golden Record to the stars

title: Kin
haiku:
bones in the dry wash
the radio sang her name
grandmother, hello
headline: Lucy, an ancient human ancestor, is discovered in Ethiopia

title: Speck
haiku:
a grain of desert
learns to hold a thought and count
the future got small
headline: Intel releases the first commercial microprocessor

title: Lo
haiku:
we typed out login
the wire failed at two letters
lo, the world began
headline: The first message is sent over ARPANET, ancestor of the internet

title: Overview
haiku:
we watched in gray light,
a man stepped onto the moon—
the whole earth held still
headline: Apollo 11 lands the first humans on the Moon

title: Borrowed
haiku:
a stranger's engine
stitched into a dying chest—
and dawn, it still beat
headline: Christiaan Barnard performs the first human heart transplant

title: Tether
haiku:
he stepped out to void,
a thread held one drifting man,
space, and he floated
headline: Alexei Leonov makes the first spacewalk

title: Splashdown
haiku:
fifteen minutes up,
a splash, a sailor's salute,
and the free world cheered
headline: Alan Shepard becomes the first American in space

title: Outbound
haiku:
one man, then the sky,
he circled the blue marble,
and came home smiling
headline: Yuri Gagarin becomes the first human to reach space

title: Bottom
haiku:
down past all sunlight,
seven miles of patient dark—
two men touched the floor
headline: The Trieste carries two men to the deepest point of the ocean

title: Shrunk
haiku:
one chip held them all
resistors, wires, whole circuits—
shrunk to fit a thumb
headline: Jack Kilby demonstrates the first integrated circuit

title: Alarm
haiku:
a beach-ball of steel
beeped once round the sleeping earth—
and nobody slept
headline: Sputnik launches, opening the Space Age

title: Deliverance
haiku:
the summers of fear—
a needle ends the long dread,
children swim again
headline: The Salk polio vaccine is declared safe and effective

title: Kinship
haiku:
two brothers, one blood,
a kidney passed like a gift—
no war in the flesh
headline: Surgeons perform the first successful kidney transplant

title: Roof
haiku:
two men cut the last
few steps to the roof of earth—
and stood in thin air
headline: Hillary and Tenzing Norgay reach the summit of Everest

title: Spiral
haiku:
two strands climbed and twined,
the code of us laid bare in
one photograph's light
headline: Watson, Crick and Franklin reveal the structure of DNA

title: Switch
haiku:
three men in a lab
coaxed a crystal to say yes—
the glass tube grew old
headline: Bell Labs demonstrates the first transistor

title: Holdout
haiku:
a fossil surfaced
flapping, blue, and very much
alive, unaware
headline: A living coelacanth, thought extinct for ages, is found alive

title: Solo
haiku:
one engine, one girl,
the ocean had no vote here—
Ireland met her wheels
headline: Amelia Earhart flies solo across the Atlantic

title: Runt
haiku:
a farm boy blinked twice
between two star-crowded plates—
one small dot had moved
headline: Clyde Tombaugh discovers Pluto

title: Bloom
haiku:
a moldy dish, left
by pure accident, on this—
death lost its footing
headline: Alexander Fleming discovers penicillin

title: Solo
haiku:
one man, one thin plane
crossed an ocean, wide awake—
Paris met the dawn
headline: Charles Lindbergh completes the first solo nonstop transatlantic flight

title: Wonders
haiku:
a candle, a crack
gold glinted back through the dust
he breathed: wonders, yes
headline: Howard Carter opens the tomb of Tutankhamun

title: Reprieve
haiku:
the wasting child slept,
a needle full of borrowed
time—and she woke thin
headline: Insulin is first used to save a young diabetic patient

title: Curvature
haiku:
the sun bent starlight,
and a patent clerk's wild guess
became the whole sky
headline: Eclipse observations confirm Einstein's general relativity

title: Compass
haiku:
five men on skis found
the bottom of the whole world—
a flag, and the wind
headline: Roald Amundsen's team reaches the South Pole

title: Heights
haiku:
clouds hid a city
stone stairs climbing to nowhere
the world looked up, up
headline: Hiram Bingham brings Machu Picchu to the world's attention

title: Crossing
haiku:
three faint dots crossed sea
no ship, no wire, only air—
the ocean got small
headline: Marconi receives the first transatlantic radio signal

title: Radiant
haiku:
she boiled tons of ore
to find a pinch that glowed blue—
night forgot to fall
headline: Marie and Pierre Curie announce the discovery of radium

title: Seethrough
haiku:
a strange light passed through
his wife's hand bloomed into bone—
the ring outlived skin
headline: Wilhelm Roentgen discovers X-rays

title: Click
haiku:
you press the button
the rest, he said, is our job
now the moment stays
headline: George Eastman patents the Kodak roll-film camera

title: Reprieve
haiku:
a boy, dog-bitten,
fourteen shots against the dark—
the boy did not turn
headline: Louis Pasteur successfully tests his rabies vaccine

title: Overtime
haiku:
a thread of carbon
burned through the night, refusing
to blink or give in
headline: Edison tests a long-lasting electric light bulb

title: Echo
haiku:
a needle traced tin
and gave the man his own voice
back to his own ear
headline: Edison announces the phonograph, recording sound for the first time

title: Summons
haiku:
come here, Watson, please—
a copper wire carried voice
and the world leaned in
headline: Alexander Graham Bell makes the first telephone call

title: Commons
haiku:
no fence, no owner
the geysers belong to all
we saved it for us
headline: Yellowstone becomes the world's first national park

title: Spike
haiku:
two rails reached for hands
a golden spike rang them shut
the coasts shook hands, done
headline: The transcontinental railroad is completed at Promontory Summit

title: Cousins
haiku:
all creatures, it turns
share one long and branching root—
we're kin to the moss
headline: Charles Darwin publishes On the Origin of Species

title: Elsewhere
haiku:
they cut, he dreamed on
a vapor closed the mind's door—
no scream, only sleep
headline: The first public demonstration of surgical anesthesia

title: Pencil
haiku:
they aimed a pencil
at one dark spot in the sky—
and found a planet
headline: Neptune is discovered, exactly where mathematics predicted

title: Key
haiku:
a slab in the mud
held three tongues saying one thing
the silence spoke back
headline: The Rosetta Stone is found, unlocking ancient hieroglyphs

title: Vacca
haiku:
a farm boy's cowpox,
one scratch, and the pox lost teeth—
we named it vaccine
headline: Edward Jenner performs the first vaccination against smallpox

title: Entourage
haiku:
four small lights in line
swung round Jupiter each night—
the earth lost its throne
headline: Galileo sees Jupiter's moons through his telescope
